sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 When David was king, there was no food for three years in a row. So David prayed to the LORD. The LORD said, "It is because of the blood Saul spilled. He and his family killed the Gibeonites."

2 So David called the Gibeonites and spoke to them. The Gibeonites were not Israelites. They were a small group left from the Amorites. The Israelites had made a promise to them. But Saul had tried to kill them because he loved Israel and Judah strongly.
3 David asked the Gibeonites, "What can I do for you? How can I make things right? Then you may bless the people of the LORD."
4 The Gibeonites said, "We do not want silver or gold from Saul's house. We do not want you to kill anyone in Israel for us." David replied, "I will do whatever you ask."
5 They answered the king, "Saul destroyed us and made plans against us. He wanted to wipe us out from every part of Israel.
6 So give us seven sons from his family. We will hang them in front of the LORD at Gibeah of Saul, the one the LORD chose." The king said, "I will give them to you."
7 But the king did not harm Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul. David had made a promise before the LORD with Jonathan, Saul's son.

8 Instead, the king took Armoni and Mephibosheth. Rizpah, the daughter of Aiah, bore these two sons to Saul. He also took the five sons of Merab, Saul's daughter. She bore them to Adriel, son of Barzillai the Meholathite.
9 David handed them over to the Gibeonites. They hanged them on the hill before the LORD. All seven died together. They were killed in the first days of the harvest, when the barley harvest began.

10 Then Rizpah, daughter of Aiah, took rough cloth and spread it on a rock. From the start of the harvest until rain fell from heaven on the bodies, she watched. She did not let birds rest on them by day. She did not let wild animals come near them by night.
11 David heard what Rizpah, Saul's second wife and daughter of Aiah, had done.

12 So David went and took the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan. He took them from the men of Jabesh-gilead. Those men had taken the bones from the open square at Beth-shan. The Philistines had hung the bodies there after they killed Saul at Gilboa.
13 David brought the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan from there. He also brought the bones of the seven men who were hanged.

14 They buried the bones of Saul and his son Jonathan in Zela in the land of Benjamin. They put them in the tomb of Saul's father Kish. They did everything the king commanded. Then God answered their prayers for the land.
15 The Philistines started war against Israel again. David and his men went down to fight them. But David grew very tired.
16 Then Ishbi-benob, one from the family of Rapha, planned to kill David. His bronze spear weighed three hundred shekels. He also carried a new sword.

17 But Abishai, son of Zeruiah, came to help David. He struck the Philistine and killed him. Then David's men made a promise to him. They said, "You must never go out with us to battle again. The light of Israel must not go out."
18 Later there was another battle with the Philistines at Gob. At that time Sibbecai the Hushathite killed Saph. Saph was one of the family of Rapha.
19 There was another battle with the Philistines at Gob. Elhanan, son of Jair the Bethlehemite, killed the brother of Goliath the Gittite. His spear pole was as big as a weaver's beam.
20 There was still another battle at Gath. A very tall man was there. He had six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot — twenty-four in all. He also came from the family of Rapha.
21 He made fun of Israel. So Jonathan, the son of David's brother Shimei, killed him.
22 So these four men from the family of Rapha in Gath died. They died at the hands of David and his men.

title: 2 Samuel 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 David sang this song to the LORD on the day the LORD saved him from all his enemies and from Saul.
2 He said: "The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer.
3 My God is my rock, in whom I find safety; he is my shield and the horn of my salvation. He is my stronghold, my refuge, and my Savior; you save me from violence.
4 I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy of praise; so I will be saved from my enemies.
5 The waves of death covered me; floods of ruin swept over me.
6 The cords of the grave wrapped around me; the traps of death stood before me.
7 In my trouble I called upon the LORD; I cried out to my God. From his temple he heard my voice; my cry for help reached his ears.
8 Then the earth shook and quaked; the foundations of the sky trembled. They shook because he burned with anger.
9 Smoke rose from his nose; burning fire came from his mouth; red-hot coals burst out.
10 He parted the sky and came down; dark clouds were under his feet.
11 He rode on a cherub and flew; he soared on the wings of the wind.
12 He made darkness a covering around him — gathered waters and thick clouds.
13 From the brightness before him coals of fire burst out.
14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the voice of the Most High rang out.
15 He shot his arrows and scattered the foes; he sent lightning and scattered them.
16 The beds of the sea appeared; the foundations of the world were laid bare at the rebuke of the LORD, at the blast of his breath.
17 He reached down from above and took hold of me; he drew me out of deep waters.
18 He rescued me from my strong enemy, from foes too mighty for me.
19 They came against me in my day of trouble, but the LORD was my support.
20 He brought me out to a wide place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
21 The LORD rewarded me for my right living; he paid me back for my clean hands.
22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD; I have not turned away from my God in sin.
23 All his rules are before me; I have not pushed his laws aside.
24 I have been blameless before him; I have kept myself from sin.
25 So the LORD has paid me back for my right living, for my clean hands in his sight.
26 To the faithful you show yourself faithful; to the blameless you show yourself blameless.
27 To the pure you show yourself pure, but to the twisted you show yourself clever.
28 You save the humble people, but your eyes are on the proud, to bring them down.
29 You, O LORD, are my lamp; the LORD lights up my darkness.
30 With you I can rush at an army; with my God I can climb a wall.
31 As for God, his way is perfect; the word of the LORD is pure. He is a shield for all who run to him for safety.
32 For who is God besides the LORD? And who is the Rock but our God?
33 God is my strong fortress; he makes my way safe.
34 He makes my feet like a deer's feet; he sets me on the high places.
35 He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
36 You have given me your shield of salvation; your gentleness lifts me high.
37 You make a wide path under me, so that my ankles do not slip.
38 I chased my enemies and destroyed them; I did not turn back until they were finished off.
39 I struck and crushed them so they could not rise; they have fallen under my feet.
40 You armed me with strength for battle; you put my enemies under me.
41 You made my enemies turn their backs to me; I finished off those who hated me.
42 They looked, but no one came to save them — to the LORD, but he did not answer.
43 I crushed them like the dust of the earth; I trampled them like mud in the streets.
44 You have saved me from the fighting of my people; you have kept me as the head of nations. A people I had not known will serve me.
45 Foreigners bow down before me; when they hear me, they obey me.
46 Foreigners lose courage; they come trembling from their hideouts.
47 The LORD lives, and blessed be my Rock! May God, the Rock of my salvation, be lifted high —
48 the God who pays back wrongs done to me and puts nations under me,
49 who frees me from my enemies. You lift me up above my foes; you rescue me from violent men.
50 Therefore I will praise you, O LORD, among the nations; I will sing praises to your name.
51 He brings great salvation to his king; he shows faithful love to his anointed king, to David and his descendants forever."

title: 2 Samuel 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 These are the last words of David: "This is the message of David son of Jesse. This is the message of the man God lifted up. He was chosen by the God of Jacob. He was the sweet singer of Israel.
2 The Spirit of the LORD spoke through me. His word was on my tongue.
3 The God of Israel spoke. The Rock of Israel said to me, 'A ruler who leads people with justice, who leads with respect for God,
4 is like the morning light at sunrise on a clear day. He is like the shining grass after rain.'
5 Is my house not right with God? He has made an everlasting agreement with me. It is set in order and safe in every part. Will he not give me my full salvation and all I want?
6 But evil people are like thorns pushed aside. No one can gather them by hand.
7 The man who touches them must use iron tools or the wooden part of a spear. Fire burns them up where they lie."
8 These are the names of David's mighty men: Josheb-basshebeth the Tahchemonite was leader of the Three. He used his spear against eight hundred men and killed them all at one time.
9 Next was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite. He was one of the three mighty men. He went with David to mock the Philistines at Pas-dammim. The men of Israel ran away,

10 but Eleazar did not move. He struck the Philistines until his hand was tired. His hand stuck to his sword. The LORD gave a great victory that day. The troops came back, but only to take things from the dead.
11 After him was Shammah son of Agee the Hararite. The Philistines gathered near a field full of lentils. Israel's troops ran away from them.
12 But Shammah stood in the middle of the field. He defended it and struck down the Philistines. So the LORD gave a great victory.
13 At harvest time, three of the thirty leaders went down to David at the cave of Adullam. A group of Philistines was camped in the Valley of Rephaim.
14 At that time David was in the strong place. The Philistine army camp was at Bethlehem.
15 David wanted water badly. He said, "Oh, I wish someone would get me water from the well by the gate of Bethlehem!"

16 So the three mighty men pushed through the Philistine camp. They drew water from the well by the gate of Bethlehem. They brought it back to David. But he would not drink it. Instead, he poured it out for the LORD.
17 He said, "Far be it from me, O LORD, to do this! Is this not the blood of the men who put their lives in danger?" So he would not drink it. These were the brave deeds of the three mighty men.
18 Abishai, the brother of Joab and son of Zeruiah, was leader of the Three. He lifted his spear against three hundred men and killed them. He became famous with the Three.
19 Was he not more honored than the Three? He became their commander, even though he was not one of the Three.
20 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a brave man from Kabzeel. He did many brave deeds. He struck down two strong fighters of Moab. On a snowy day, he went down into a pit and killed a lion.

21 He also killed an Egyptian, a huge man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand. But Benaiah went against him with a club. He grabbed the spear from his hand and killed the Egyptian with his own spear.
22 These were the brave deeds of Benaiah son of Jehoiada. He became famous with the three mighty men.
23 He was most honored among the Thirty, but he did not become one of the Three. David put him in charge of his guards.
24 These were members of the Thirty: Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
25 Shammah the Harodite, Elika the Harodite,
26 Helez the Paltite, Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite,
27 Abiezer the Anathothite, Mebunnai the Hushathite,
28 Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite,
29 Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite, Ittai son of Ribai from Gibeah of the Benjamites,
30 Benaiah the Pirathonite, Hiddai from the brooks of Gaash,
31 Abi-albon the Arbathite, Azmaveth the Barhumite,
32 Eliahba the Shaalbonite, the sons of Jashen,
33 Jonathan son of Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam son of Sharar the Hararite,
34 Eliphelet son of Ahasbai the Maacathite, Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite,
35 Hezro the Carmelite, Paarai the Arbite,
36 Igal son of Nathan of Zobah, Bani the Gadite,
37 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,
38 Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
39 and Uriah the Hittite. There were thirty-seven in all.

title: 2 Samuel 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 Again the LORD became angry with Israel. He moved David to act against them. He said, "Go and count the people of Israel and Judah."
2 So the king said to Joab, the leader of his army, "Go now through all the tribes of Israel from Dan to Beersheba. Count the soldiers, so I may know their number."
3 But Joab replied to the king, "May the LORD your God add to the soldiers a hundred times more. May my lord the king see it with his own eyes. But why does my lord the king want to do this?"
4 Yet the king's word was stronger than Joab and the army leaders. So Joab and the army leaders left the king to count the soldiers of Israel.
5 They crossed the Jordan and camped near Aroer. The camp was south of the town in the middle of the valley. They went on toward Gad and Jazer.
6 Then they went to Gilead and the land of Tahtim-hodshi. They went on to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon.
7 They went toward the strong city of Tyre and all the towns of the Hivites and Canaanites. At last they went to the Negev of Judah, to Beersheba.
8 After nine months and twenty days they had gone through the whole land. Then they returned to Jerusalem.
9 Joab told the king the total number of the soldiers. In Israel there were 800,000 strong men who could fight with the sword. In Judah there were 500,000.

10 After David had counted the soldiers, his heart troubled him. He said to the LORD, "I have sinned greatly in what I have done. Now, O LORD, please take away the sin of your servant. I have acted very foolishly."
11 When David got up in the morning, the word of the LORD came to Gad the prophet, David's seer.
12 The LORD said, "Go and tell David: 'I am giving you three choices. Choose one of them, and I will do it to you.'"

13 So Gad went to David and said, "Will you choose three years of no food in your land? Or three months of running from your enemies? Or three days of sickness in your land? Now think it over and tell me how to answer the One who sent me."
14 David answered Gad, "I am in great trouble. Please let us fall into the hand of the LORD. His mercy is very great. But do not let me fall into the hands of men."
15 So the LORD sent a sickness on Israel from that morning until the set time. Seventy thousand people from Dan to Beersheba died.

16 But when the angel reached out his hand to destroy Jerusalem, the LORD felt sorry and stopped the harm. He said to the angel who was destroying the people, "Enough! Pull back your hand now!" At that time the angel of the LORD was by the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.

17 When David saw the angel striking down the people, he said to the LORD, "Surely I, the shepherd, have sinned and done wrong. But these sheep, what have they done? Please let your hand fall on me and my father's house."
18 That day Gad came to David and said, "Go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite."
19 So David went up, just as Gad spoke and the LORD had commanded.
20 When Araunah looked out, he saw the king and his servants coming toward him. He went out and bowed with his face to the ground before the king.
21 Araunah said, "Why has my lord the king come to his servant?" David replied, "To buy your threshing floor, so I may build an altar to the LORD. Then the sickness on the people may stop."
22 Araunah said to David, "My lord the king may take what seems good and offer it. Here are the oxen for a burnt offering. Here are the tools and the wood for the fire.
23 O king, Araunah gives all these to the king." He also said to the king, "May the LORD your God accept you."

24 "No," the king replied, "I must pay a price. I will not offer burnt offerings to the LORD my God that cost me nothing." So David bought the threshing floor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.
25 There he built an altar to the LORD. He offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. Then the LORD answered the prayers for the land, and the sickness on Israel stopped.
